channel.title: Blaugust 2022 on Chasing Dings!
channel.description: Recent content in Blaugust 2022 on Chasing Dings!

item.title: Creating "32 Bullets" -- an AI adventure.
item.description: In “32 Bullets”, you play as a professional hitman with only 32 bullets to complete 32 contracts. The catch is that you must complete them all within a certain amount of time.

item.title: Blaugust Hath 31 Days
item.description:
Genre: 2D Platformer. Title: “Blaugust Hath 31 Days”. Outline: In this 2D platformer, you play as a young woman who is terrorized by 31 different monsters, one for each night of August. You must use your agility and wit to survive until morning and defeat the monsters once and for all.

The game is set in a small town that is being terrorized by 31 different monsters, one for each night of August. The player must use their agility and wit to survive until morning and defeat the monsters once and for all.

item.title: Blaugust theme week: Lessons learned
item.description:
I spent the 31 days of Blaugust working to see if I could get AI to show some creativity in game design, and if I could get AI image generation programs to produce the art I want at a price I could afford. I learned some things.

I used OpenAI’s GPT-3 “text-davinci-002” text completion tool. I chose this because most of the alarm over how the rise of AI would be the death of writing tended to surround this particular tool.

item.title: The Golden Years: 30 Something
item.description: The Golden Years: 30 Something is a life simulation game that highlights the importance of balancing work, life, and everything in between. The game features multiple different endings depending on the choices you make throughout the game.

item.title: Blaugust 2022: Staying (un)Motivated
item.description: Keeping a blog alive is a lot of pressure. Wouldn’t it make more sense to have a central Blaugust blog that everyone could post in, rather than having a hundred small blogs?

item.title: Leap Year: the 29th Day
item.description: After the world disappears on Leap Day, you must team up with the only other person left awake to find out what happened.

item.title: The 28th Amendment -- a game about the USA Constitution
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 28th: “The 28th Amendment”.

“The 28th Amendment” is a political simulation game in which the player is tasked with drafting and ratifying a new amendment to the United States Constitution. The player can choose from a variety of topics, including gun control, abortion, and same-sex marriage.

item.title: The 27th Chamber: A Game about Time
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 27th: “The 27th Chamber”.

Number: 27. Genre: Metroidvania. Title: “The 27th Chamber”. Outline: You play as a member of an elite group of soldiers who are sent on a mission to investigate a strange energy signature emanating from an abandoned research facility.

item.title: 1926: The Golden Age of Aviation
item.description: Experience the golden age of aviation in this flight simulator set in the 1920s.

item.title: Blaugust 2022: Dev Appreciation Week
item.description: We highlight some of the great turn-based RPGs that are currently in development by independent developers. These are games that are being made with passion and care, and it shows in the quality of the games. If you’re a fan of turn-based RPGs, be sure to check out these games!

item.title: Blaugust 2022: Introduce Yourself week
item.description: Welcome to Blaugust week 1, where I introduce myself! Or some version of myself, anyway.

item.title: 25 Cents: The Age of the Arcade
item.description: In 25 Cents, players take on the role of arcade owners in the 1980s, running their businesses and competing against each other to attract high scores from players who drop quarters into virtual machines.

item.title: 24/7: Attention, Undead Shoppers edition.
item.description: Do you have what it takes to keep the undead under control? Find out in 24/7, a challenging and exciting first-person shooter with a horror twist. Patrol the mall at night and keep the creatures of the night in check. Use a variety of different weapons and tools to help you in your task. Can you survive until dawn?

item.title: 23 Fathoms: A Cold War Era Submarine Simulator
item.description:
Number: 23. Genre: Submarine Simulator. Title: “23 Fathoms”. Outline: In this submarine simulator, you are the captain of a nuclear submarine on a secret mission. You must navigate the treacherous waters, avoid detection, and complete your mission without being detected.

In the grand tradition of Silent Hunter, “23 Fathoms” puts you in the captain’s chair of a nuclear submarine. As you navigate the treacherous waters, you must avoid detection while completing your mission. The game features realistic submarine physics and gameplay, as well as a dynamic campaign that changes based on your choices.

item.title: "The 22nd Letter": A Game of Mystery and Scrabble
item.description: The 22nd Letter is a game of mystery and Scrabble. As you play the game, you’ll gradually uncover the story of “V”, a man who disappeared during a Scrabble tournament long ago. Can you solve the mystery?

item.title: 21 Reactions: Chemical Chaos
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 21st: “21 Reactions: Chemical Chaos”.

Number: 21. Genre: Rhythm game. Title: “21 Grams”. Outline: In this rhythm game, you must keep the beat while 21 grams of various substances are added to a beaker. The challenge lies in ensuring that the mixture does not explode.

item.title: The Wild 20s - The City is Your Sandbox
item.description: “The Wild 20s” is an open-world sandbox game set in the Roaring Twenties. You can explore the city, find jobs, start businesses, get into trouble, or just enjoy the nightlife. It’s up to you!

item.title: Can You Make It To The End Of 19th Street In This Frantic Endless Runner?
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 19th: “19th Street Dash”.

You dallied a little too long over your coffee at the Mug & Cup, and now you’re going to be late for your interview crosstown. You bolt out the door and start sprinting down 19th Street. You have to be careful not to trip on cracks in the sidewalk, jump over obstacles in your path, and be mindful of the gaps between buildings.

item.title: Death Dealer 18 Wheeler
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 18th: “Death Dealer 18 Wheeler”.

Number: 18. Genre: Tower defense game. Title: “Death Dealer 18 Wheeler”. Outline: You are in charge of a 18-wheeler truck and must deliver your load safely while fending off attacks from bandits.

item.title: There is No Room 17 in Kaisei Academy
item.description: “There Is No Room 17 in Kaisei Academy” is a fun and mystery-filled visual novel that is perfect for fans of the genre.

item.title: The Escape of King Louis XVI
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 16th: “The 16th King”.

Number: 16. Genre: Text-based adventure game. Title: “The 16th King” Outline: King Louis XVI was the 16 King Louis and the final king of France. You play as the king during the French Revolution, and must escape from prison and flee to safety.

item.title: The 15th Element: A Squad-Based Shooter With Puzzle Elements
item.description: The 15th Element is a squad-based first person shooter with puzzle elements, set in an alien world. The game includes a variety of DLC options, such as a new enemy race and a survival-style mode.

item.title: The 14 Pages of Sarah Woods
item.description: The Number 14: A Touching Game with Excellent Writing and Interesting Characters

item.title: Thirteen Step Program: A Psychological Thriller
item.description: You play as a young woman who is struggling with addiction. After hitting rock bottom, you enter into recovery and start attending AA meetings. However, you quickly discover that not all is as it seems within the group…

item.title: Twelve Angry Zoos
item.description: “Twelve Angry Zoos” is a fast-paced arcade game that will test your reflexes and puzzle-solving skills. With 12 different zoos in 12 different cities, there’s plenty of gameplay to keep you entertained.

item.title: 11/11/11: The Thousand Year Second
item.description: “11/11/11: The Thousand Year Second” is a turn-based strategy game about time, relationships, and choices.

item.title: 10 Million B.C.: An open-world action RPG set in the time of the dinosaurs
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 10th: “10 Million B.C.”.

Number: 10. Genre: Open-world action RPG. Title: “10 Million B.C.” Outline: In this open-world action RPG set in the time of the dinosaurs, you play as a young hunter who must survive in a hostile world filled with giant beasts. Tame dinosaurs and use them to help you travel across the vast landscape and discover lost civilizations.

item.title: The Nine-Lives Chronicles
item.description: You play as a young woman who discovers she has nine lives. Along with eight other people who also have nine lives, you must use your extra lives to help others and make the world a better place.

item.title: The Amazing Adventures of 8-Ball!
item.description: “Adventure of 8-Ball” is a 2D platformer mobile game with 50 levels, attractive graphics, and catchy music. The player controls 8-ball, a little black cat, on a quest to save their owner from the evil dog catcher who has kidnapped them.

item.title: The Seven Sages: A Masterclass in Metroidvania Design
item.description: In “The Seven Sages”, you play as one of the seven ancient sages of mystery, who must journey through a vast and dangerous world to gather the seven magical artifacts that will save the realm from a great evil.

item.title: The Six Cats of Madame Miracle
item.description: The Six Cats of Madame Miracle is a light-hearted and fun visual novel that is perfect for players of all ages. The puzzles are easy to learn and solve, and the game’s cute art style and cheerful music make it a joy to play.

item.title: Steve, the Fifth Horseman of the Apocalypse
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 5th: “Steve, the Fifth Horseman of the Apocalypse”.

This is a game where you play as Steve, the fifth horseman of the Apocalypse, who was left behind when the other four horsemen rode off to bring about the end of the world. With only your trusty steed and a few weapons, you must now ride across the country, fighting off hordes of zombies and other monsters that have taken over the world.

item.title: Fourt Knight: An Awesome Game That's Worth the Price
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 4th: “Fourt Knight”.

“Fourt Knight” is an action adventure game where you play a lone knight who must save the kingdom from a dark force.

The game is set in a medieval world and you will use your sword and shield to fight your way through hordes of enemies. You will also use your bow to take down enemy archers. There are puzzles to solve and secret areas to explore.

item.title: Three Frow: A deep, tactical basketball RPG
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 3rd: “Three Frow”.

In this tactical strategy sports game, you must recruit and play the best basketball players from around the world in order to win the NBA championship. Cheering crowds will be your constant companion on your rise to the top. These aren’t your ordinary crowds, though — if you make a bad play, expect the fans to boo you mercilessly, or even throw things at you. Make a great play or a stunning assist, though, and the fans may just carry you off the court on their shoulders.

item.title: 2Dark 2C: A Unique Spin on Traditional RTS Gameplay
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 2nd: “2Dark 2C”.

In this complex real-time strategy computer game you play a spacefaring vampire attacking colonies on planets ranging from swamps to desert worlds with Medusas as mid-bosses and aliens comprised entirely of flower petals as final bosses… added bonus: instead of gathering resources conventionally, suck all available blood reserve dry (although keep essential personnel fed mightily so your ship doesn’t grind to a halt) for much quicker growth plus veterancy effects / unit upgrades!

item.title: One: The Game - A Superheroic Multiplayer Experience
item.description:
I asked OpenAI’s GPT-3 to generate 31 different videogame ideas, one for each day of Blaugust, each dealing in some way with that particular day of the month. Here’s its idea for August 1st.

“One: The Game” is an online multiplayer game where you play as one of a group of super-powered beings who must work together to stop a madman from taking over the world.

The game is played in a third-person perspective, and you can choose to play as one of several different characters, each with their own unique abilities.

item.title: Welcome to Blaugust 2022: 31 game ideas from AI
item.description: This article is about the author’s plan to use AI to help write their blog posts for Blaugust.
